title: Denise Handicap

jokes.text: I'm seeing her in a private recital at a friend's house. - Ooh! - I love the sound of envy. It's music to my ears.
jokes.setup: Larry brags about getting to see a violinist privately
jokes.punchline: Music to my ears - literal and figurative
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning that feels natural to the character and situation

jokes.text: You're halfway decent looking. I think we could arrange it. - Wow, thanks. - Yeah. It's those compliments that just keep me going.
jokes.setup: Denise gives a backhanded compliment about Larry's looks
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic response about the 'compliment'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Larry's self-deprecating wit

jokes.text: You're not some creepo creep, are you? I am. I'm a total creep.
jokes.setup: Denise asks if Larry is a creep
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately admits he is
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David honesty that's both disarming and funny

jokes.text: If you deliver bad news like it's bad news, it's gonna be bad news. If you deliver bad news like it's not a big deal, then it's not a big deal.
jokes.setup: Larry explains his technique for delivering bad news
jokes.punchline: The circular logic of the explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry pseudo-wisdom that sounds logical but is absurd

jokes.text: It's like you're in the hospital, the doctor comes in and says, 'oh good, nice shades. You have terminal cancer.' That's exactly my point. This is how a doctor should do it.
jokes.setup: Larry continues explaining casual bad news delivery
jokes.punchline: Casual terminal cancer diagnosis as an example
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation to an absurd extreme with perfect Larry logic

jokes.text: And by the way, susie, I so want to divorce you.
jokes.setup: Jeff demonstrates Larry's casual bad news technique
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions wanting a divorce
jokes.explanation: Perfect application of Larry's theory in practice

jokes.text: Once she wheeled out from the table... I... Come on, what could I do? I'm taking her out tonight.
jokes.setup: Larry explains why he asked out the wheelchair woman
jokes.punchline: He was trapped by social obligation after she revealed her disability
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb setup showing Larry's social awkwardness and misguided attempts at being appropriate

jokes.text: What gets priority on a check, asker or toucher? What is he, judge judy?
jokes.setup: Larry and Rosie fight over who pays the check
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exasperated Judge Judy reference
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to Larry's absurd social rules

jokes.text: Hello, kelsey. Kelsey. Kelsey grammer. - Frasier. - Just kelsey. We don't call her frasier.
jokes.setup: Larry meets their adopted Chinese daughter named Kelsey
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately makes the Kelsey Grammer/Frasier connection
jokes.explanation: Predictable reference but fits Larry's character perfectly

jokes.text: Have you noticed if she has any proclivity for chopsticks? - Why? - Why would she have a proclivity for chopsticks? - Well, she's chinese.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about the Chinese daughter's chopstick skills
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact racist assumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David racial insensitivity delivered with complete sincerity

jokes.text: if you took an american kid who's never used chopsticks before and a chinese kid who's never used chopsticks before, would the chinese kid do better? - So the american kid is the control group in... - She uses a fork.
jokes.setup: Larry poses his chopsticks theory as a scientific experiment
jokes.punchline: John treating it like a real study, then deflating it
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity while showing Larry's pseudo-intellectual approach to racism

jokes.text: First time she uses chopsticks, give me a call. Just let me know. Give me a call and say, 'you wouldn't believe it. This kid's amazing.'
jokes.setup: Larry continues his chopsticks obsession
jokes.punchline: He wants a phone call to confirm his racist theory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Larry's inability to let go of bad ideas

jokes.text: do you have any concerns that one of the biological parents could be a psychotic? I'm just saying. You don't want to have a schizophrenic on your hands.
jokes.setup: Larry continues inappropriate questions about their adoption
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting the child might be mentally ill
jokes.explanation: More classic Larry inappropriate behavior escalating the cringe

jokes.text: So the American kid is the control group in...? / She uses a fork.
jokes.setup: Larry's elaborate chopstick experiment proposal
jokes.punchline: Parents' matter-of-fact responses deflate Larry's theorizing
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man responses that deflate Larry's elaborate setup with simple reality.

jokes.text: If we're gonna have a second date, you're gonna have to get an electric chair. I'm not doing this again.
jokes.setup: Larry carried Denise up the stairs to the restaurant
jokes.punchline: He demands she get motorized equipment for future dates
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Larry David logic - making HER accommodation HIS demand

jokes.text: Lift with your legs. - I am lifting with my legs. - Don't carry it all on your back.
jokes.setup: Larry is struggling to carry Denise up the stairs
jokes.punchline: She's giving him lifting advice while being carried
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy with role reversal

jokes.text: Larry, hurry up. - Hurry up? - Hurry up. - Shut up!
jokes.setup: Denise tells Larry to hurry while he's carrying her
jokes.punchline: Larry snaps and tells her to shut up
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the physical comedy tension

jokes.text: Jesus, that really got my back. Yeah. I feel it radiating down my leg. How am I gonna play golf tomorrow?
jokes.setup: Larry just helped Denise by carrying her up stairs
jokes.punchline: His immediate self-centered complaint about the inconvenience to him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry selfishness - immediately making himself the victim after helping someone.

jokes.text: Generally I'm just walking from one place to sit to the other place to sit. Yeah, but I'm not... You can't classify me as a walker.
jokes.setup: Larry explains he doesn't walk much
jokes.punchline: His insistence that he can't be classified as a walker
jokes.explanation: Good Larry taxonomy obsession and self-awareness

jokes.text: The thing is that when... When we first met you were wearing a hat. And then you came to the door d I was like, 'who's this? Oh oh!' because you are bald.
jokes.setup: Denise explains why she seemed disappointed when Larry arrived
jokes.punchline: She didn't know he was bald and was surprised
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with the tables turned on Larry

jokes.text: I get it. I had a hat on and then baldy showed up.
jokes.setup: Larry processes Denise's baldness revelation
jokes.punchline: He calls himself 'baldy'
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating response that shows Larry's self-awareness

jokes.text: Have you ever considered plugs? - Plugs? Plugs? You mention plugs to me? You mention... You sit at this table and mention plugs?
jokes.setup: Denise suggests Larry get hair plugs
jokes.punchline: Larry's outraged reaction to the suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing the hypocrisy - she can comment on his disability but he can't on hers

jokes.text: Yeah, I didn't know when you wheeled away from the table that... - Mm-hmm, that I'm a wheelie. - That you're a wheelie.
jokes.setup: Larry explains he didn't know about her disability initially
jokes.punchline: Denise coins the term 'wheelie' and Larry adopts it
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that will become a callback term

jokes.text: By the way, what's the proper term? Can I say handicapped? What is it? Are you disabled? Handicapped? Challenged? Are you challenged? - Right now I am, yeah.
jokes.setup: Larry asks about proper terminology for her condition
jokes.punchline: Denise says she's challenged right now (by him)
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that turns Larry's PC anxiety against him

jokes.text: Um, you know, I should probably... I should probably get going. Right. Because I'm handicapped? What? No, of course not. What are you saying that for?
jokes.setup: Larry tries to leave without coming in
jokes.punchline: Denise immediately assumes it's because of her disability
jokes.explanation: Good trap for Larry using his own guilt against him

jokes.text: Jamie: is that him? He's a fucking asshole! John: I think it would be best for everyone if you didn't come to the chee-yun concert. Jamie: I don't want him anywhere near kelsey!
jokes.setup: Voicemail from the adoptive parents
jokes.punchline: They're disinviting Larry from the concert because of his comments about their daughter
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing consequences of Larry's earlier behavior

jokes.text: How was the date with that wheelchair chick? - I don't kiss and tell. - Come on, man, what happened? Well, I'll just give you the bare bones of it, okay?
jokes.setup: Leon asks about the date
jokes.punchline: Larry says he'll give the 'bare bones' (sexual innuendo)
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo that sets up the sex conversation

jokes.text: Yeah, and there was like no reaction at all. And I don't know if it's because I was bad and didn't know what I was doing, or if it's just her condition.
jokes.setup: Larry describes the sexual encounter
jokes.punchline: He can't tell if he's bad at sex or if it's her paralysis
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awful Larry David sexual insecurity mixed with disability insensitivity

jokes.text: If you fucking brung it, if you really fucking brung it, man, it doesn't matter what the fuck is wrong with her... Coma, fucking handicapped, any of that shit.
jokes.setup: Leon responds to Larry's sexual performance anxiety
jokes.punchline: Even a coma patient would respond to good sex
jokes.explanation: Leon's outrageous sexual braggadocio escalating the disability humor

jokes.text: You did the dizzle on her, right? - I did what? - The dizzle. - The dizzle? You did your dizzle on her, right? Yeah, I did my dizzle.
jokes.setup: Leon asks if Larry did the 'dizzle'
jokes.punchline: Larry doesn't know what it means but claims he did it
jokes.explanation: Good Leon slang that Larry pretends to understand

jokes.text: I would've had her ass tap-dancing, larry. I don't fuck around. Give me her goddamn number. I'll go over there and twist that ass up, larry. I'll bend that bitch like beckham.
jokes.setup: Leon brags about his sexual prowess
jokes.punchline: Multiple sexual metaphors including 'bend that bitch like beckham'
jokes.explanation: Leon's increasingly outrageous sexual metaphors with the Beckham reference being particularly clever

jokes.text: I'll bend that bitch like beckham.
jokes.setup: Leon continuing his crude boasts
jokes.punchline: Pop culture reference applied to sexual bragging
jokes.explanation: Standard pop culture reference humor - not particularly original but fits Leon's character.

jokes.text: You can't break up with a handicapped person by phone, right? No, huh-uh. No, you got to do it face to face.
jokes.setup: Larry wants to break up with Denise
jokes.punchline: Even Leon agrees you can't break up with a disabled person by phone
jokes.explanation: Good social rule that even the inappropriate Leon acknowledges

jokes.text: Just park in the handicapped spot in front of the restaurant. Handicapped spot? How am I gonna do that? This is how. Like this.
jokes.setup: They can't find parking
jokes.punchline: Denise gets Larry to park in the handicapped spot by getting out
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag showing the practical advantages of dating someone with a disability

jokes.text: Hi. I'm sorry, it's gonna be about 45 minutes. Oh, table for two? - Mm-hmm. - great. Right this way, please. - Really? - Absolutely.
jokes.setup: Restaurant has a 45-minute wait
jokes.punchline: They immediately get seated when hostess sees Denise in wheelchair
jokes.explanation: Good visual showing more practical benefits of the wheelchair

jokes.text: And we're gonna start you off with a couple of glasses of champagne. Compliments of the house. - You do this for everyone? - No, we don't.
jokes.setup: They get free champagne
jokes.punchline: Larry confirms it's because of the wheelchair
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of wheelchair perks with Larry's typical directness

jokes.text: Oh! Hi, how are you? - Hi. - Nice to see you. - Nice to meet you. Yeah, nice to meet you too. Very nice. Very nice. Little surprising, but nice.
jokes.setup: Ted meets Denise for the first time
jokes.punchline: His awkward admission that her wheelchair is 'surprising'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how people react to disability

jokes.text: Just have one bite. Have a bite, come on. I'm not in the mood, ted. I don't want it... You're making me look like an asshole... Be a fucking friend, will you please?
jokes.setup: Ted insists Larry try the pie he bought
jokes.punchline: Ted's increasingly desperate and profane pleading
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how small social gestures become battles

jokes.text: Take this fucking piece of pie and get it out of my face. Put the fucking pie down! Don't put that pie down! Do not put that pie down! God damn it!
jokes.setup: The pie argument reaches a crescendo
jokes.punchline: Competing commands about the pie placement
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation to absurdity over something trivial

jokes.text: You know something? I heard rosie o'donnell beat the crap out of you. Oh, really? That's bullshit. That's what I heard. Rosie beat the crap out of you... You big sissy. You big fucking sissy.
jokes.setup: Ted escalates the pie fight
jokes.punchline: Brings up the Rosie O'Donnell fight and calls Larry a sissy
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the current conflict

jokes.text: Oh, what a lovely night for a stroll and a roll. That's right.
jokes.setup: Larry and Denise walking/rolling after dinner
jokes.punchline: Larry's terrible pun about stroll and roll
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry bad pun that shows his insensitive but well-meaning nature

jokes.text: These are the people who disinvited us to the concert... We would love to have both of you. We misjudged you... We will absolutely give you the best seat in the house. No question. Or do you already have it?
jokes.setup: The adoptive parents re-invite Larry to the concert
jokes.punchline: Jamie's terrible joke about Denise already having the best seat
jokes.explanation: Awkward attempt at wheelchair humor that falls flat

jokes.text: We would love to have both of you. / We misjudged you. / Both of you please come.
jokes.setup: The couple who disinvited Larry seeing him with Denise
jokes.punchline: Their complete reversal due to his perceived moral character
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Larry's dating situation rehabilitates his social standing despite his unchanged character.

jokes.text: Get up off your fat ass and let's take a walk! It'll be romantic!
jokes.setup: Susie wants Jeff to take a romantic walk
jokes.punchline: She calls him fat-ass while suggesting romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Susie character moment - abusive while trying to be romantic

jokes.text: I don't have her number or her address... She was in my blackberry under denise handicap. That's how I remember these names... I've got shawn yoga... Teresa masseuse.
jokes.setup: Larry lost Denise's contact info
jokes.punchline: He saved her as 'Denise Handicap'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry David cataloging system that's logical but horrifyingly insensitive

jokes.text: Like right here I got... Look look. Nancy big tits. I know nancy's got big ass tits. Jonel sweet ass. That's the only way I know who the fuck it is.
jokes.setup: Leon agrees with Larry's contact naming system
jokes.punchline: His even cruder examples
jokes.explanation: Leon's characteristically crude escalation of Larry's system

jokes.text: When shit is too clean you can't fucking tell where the fuck you at. You know what I mean? Is that her right there? No, that's not her. But you know what? They might know each other... Handicapped people know other handicapped people, right?
jokes.setup: They're looking for Denise in the neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Assumption that disabled people all know each other
jokes.explanation: Good observation about minority assumptions with typical Larry logic

jokes.text: If you were living in an all-white neighborhood and there was a brother, you would know the brother, right? - I would know the brother. I would go holla at him.
jokes.setup: Larry uses racial analogy for his disability theory
jokes.punchline: Leon agrees he'd know the other Black person
jokes.explanation: Interesting racial/disability parallel that both characters accept

jokes.text: Handicapped people know other handicapped people, right? / You would think. If you were living in an all-white neighborhood and there was a brother, you would know the brother, right?
jokes.setup: Larry assuming disabled people all know each other
jokes.punchline: Leon's racial analogy that makes the assumption sound absurd
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy that exposes Larry's assumption while being equally problematic. Leon's logic is perfect.

jokes.text: Denise and me and stephan hawking, we hang out all the time, 'cause we all live in the wheelchair neighborhood. No, I'm sorry. I don't know your friend.
jokes.setup: Larry assumes she knows Denise because they're both in wheelchairs
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response about wheelchair neighborhood and Stephen Hawking
jokes.explanation: Brilliant sarcastic takedown of Larry's assumptions with perfect Stephen Hawking reference

jokes.text: You need some... You need a push? - No. - I'm a good pusher. - Yeah, you're pushy all right.
jokes.setup: Larry offers to push her wheelchair
jokes.punchline: She turns 'pusher' into 'pushy'
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay that also describes Larry perfectly

jokes.text: Well, assholes don't go out with the disabled, okay? Check that out. Yeah, she's my date. - That girl is your date? - That girl is my date. - In the chair? - In the chair.
jokes.setup: Larry defends himself against being called an asshole
jokes.punchline: Uses dating Denise as proof he's not an asshole
jokes.explanation: Good Larry logic - using his disability dating as moral defense

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? Denise is here? / What? / Fuck.
jokes.setup: Larry at concert with new wheelchair date while his actual date is there
jokes.punchline: Jeff realizing Larry's about to be exposed
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the climactic confrontation - Jeff's horror at Larry's situation.

jokes.text: Wendy wheelchair? Hello? What? You're in the closet? Hang on.
jokes.setup: Larry's phone rings during the concert with contact name visible
jokes.punchline: Everyone sees 'Wendy wheelchair' and she's calling from hiding
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to the naming system causing maximum embarrassment

jokes.text: Who the hell is she? Wendy wheelchair. Who is she? Denise handicap.
jokes.setup: The two women confront Larry about each other
jokes.punchline: He identifies them by their contact names
jokes.explanation: Perfect convergence of Larry's naming system and romantic disaster

jokes.text: It's not my fault... It was an honest mistake. We may be disabled, but you're disgusting. Get back here, larry. You get back here!
jokes.setup: Larry tries to defend himself from upstairs
jokes.punchline: The women deliver final moral judgment while unable to reach him
jokes.explanation: Good ending showing the moral tables turned with physical advantage to Larry

tmdb_overview: Larry believes that he has found a way to enjoy someone else's disability without being disabled himself. Meanwhile, he hopes to end a rumor that Rosie O'Donnell can beat him up.
